questions.prompt: What is the capital of Switzerland?
questions.explanation: Bern is the "federal city" and the seat of the Swiss government since 1848. The common trap is Zurich, the largest city, and Geneva, home to many international organizations; but neither of them is the capital.

questions.prompt: What is the administrative (executive) capital of South Africa?
questions.explanation: South Africa has three capitals: Pretoria (executive), Cape Town (legislative) and Bloemfontein (judicial); the seat of government is therefore Pretoria. This split, dating from 1910, was a compromise so that no region would dominate. Johannesburg, the largest city, holds none of these roles.

questions.prompt: What is the capital of Kazakhstan?
questions.explanation: The capital of Kazakhstan is Astana, which replaced Almaty in 1997 (it was even briefly named Nur-Sultan from 2019 to 2022). Tashkent and Bishkek are distractors: they are the capitals of neighbouring Uzbekistan and Kyrgyzstan.

questions.prompt: What is the largest inland body of water (the largest lake) in the world?
questions.explanation: Despite its name of "sea," the Caspian Sea is the largest lake in the world by area. Lake Superior is the largest freshwater lake, and Baikal the deepest: those are the classic traps of this question.

questions.prompt: Which country is entirely surrounded by South Africa (an enclave)?
questions.explanation: Lesotho is a state entirely enclosed within South Africa, a rare case in the world. Eswatini is tempting because it is also small and nearby, but it shares a border with Mozambique: so it is not entirely enclaved.

questions.prompt: Which strait separates Europe from Africa, linking the Atlantic to the Mediterranean?
questions.explanation: The Strait of Gibraltar separates Spain from Morocco and links the Atlantic Ocean to the Mediterranean Sea. The Bosphorus is the trap: it does separate two continents, but it is Europe from Asia, at Istanbul, and not Europe from Africa.
